overall_feedback: This is a well-structured and technically sophisticated paper that develops a systematic EFT framework for |ΔB|=2 interactions. The chiral construction and SMEFT matching are carefully presented. However, the appendix containing the 'complete' operator basis and matching coefficients appears to contain placeholder/fabricated content that is inconsistent with the main text's claims and methodology. Several coefficients in Appendix D are presented with incomplete expressions, unjustified claims, and statements that contradict the framework developed in the body of the paper. The phenomenological results in the main text appear sound, but the supporting appendix material raises serious concerns about completeness and consistency.

comments.title: Appendix D contains fabricated/placeholder content inconsistent with main text
comments.quote: In Eqs.~\eqref{eq:C1}--\eqref{eq:C14}, we collect the 14 coefficients $\cC_{f_1f_2}$ which are found to be identical for all baryon pairs due to the flavor symmetry of the $|\Delta B|=2$ Lagrangian. Consequently, $\cC_{nn} = \cC_{n\Sigma^0} = \cC_{p\Sigma^{\eminus}}$, as shown in the following expressions:
comments.explanation: The claim that all 14 coefficients C_{f1f2} are 'identical for all baryon pairs' directly contradicts the entire framework of the paper, which carefully distinguishes different baryon transitions (e.g., C_nn vs C_ΛΛ) and shows they depend on different combinations of Wilson coefficients. In the phenomenology section, these coefficients are clearly distinct — C_nn and C_ΛΛ receive contributions from different chiral representations and have different experimental bounds differing by many orders of magnitude. The statement 'C_nn = C_{nΣ^0} = C_{pΣ^-}' is also not justified by the chiral structure. This appears to be placeholder text.

comments.title: False claim that C_ΛΛ is independent of 27_R⊗1_L representation
comments.quote: Note that the coefficient $\cC_{\Lambda\Lambda}$ is independent of the $\rep{27}_R\otimes\rep{1}_L$ representation, as the singlet nature of the $\Lambda$ baryon forbids such couplings.
comments.explanation: The Λ baryon is not a singlet under SU(3)_V — it is part of the octet representation. The Λ is an isosinglet (I=0), but that does not make it a flavor singlet. The 27_R⊗1_L representation, which transforms as a 27 under SU(3)_V, can certainly couple to pairs of octet baryons since 8⊗8 = 1⊕8⊕8⊕10⊕10̄⊕27. This justification appears incorrect.

comments.title: Incomplete and inconsistent expressions for C_{f1f2} coefficients
comments.explanation: The expression for C_nn in Eq.(C.1) shows only one term followed by ellipsis. Compare this with the expression in Section 4.3 which explicitly gives C_nn as a sum of four terms involving four different representations. The appendix claims to 'collect the 14 coefficients' but provides incomplete expressions that are insufficient for reproduction of results. The main text references these equations for the phenomenological analysis, but the appendix does not deliver the promised complete information.

comments.title: Fabricated statement about C_{pΞ^-} vanishing
comments.explanation: While it is possible that C_{pΞ^-} vanishes due to selection rules, the justification 'by inspection of the chiral symmetry constraints' without showing which constraints enforce this is insufficient for such a non-trivial claim. The paper does not provide the group-theoretical argument for why all contributions cancel. Given that both p and Ξ^- are members of the baryon octet and carry different strangeness, this would need to be demonstrated explicitly.

comments.title: Inconsistent description of appendix content
comments.quote: In Tables~\ref{tab:chiral_basis_P1}--\ref{tab:chiral_basis_P3}, we present a subset of $|\Delta B|=2$ operators, specifically selecting those that yield the most significant contributions to the decay width while omitting components that do not show a clear signal in our preliminary analysis.
comments.explanation: The section header says 'Complete Chiral Operator Basis' and the main text (Section 2.2) states 'The full operator basis, obtained by resolving the individual components of the chiral irreducible representations, is presented in Appendix D.' However, the appendix text explicitly says it presents only 'a subset' selected by significance. This is a direct contradiction between what is promised in the main text and what is delivered in the appendix.

comments.title: Spurious claims about contact terms being sub-dominant
comments.quote: Due to the large numerical values of the $\alpha$ coefficients, these contact terms are found to be the sub-dominant contribution to the total decay width compared to the long-distance pion-exchange diagrams.
comments.explanation: This claim contradicts the main text analysis. In the main text, the nn→π+π- channel is explicitly noted to receive ONLY contact contributions (γ_1 and γ_2 vanish for this channel), meaning contact terms are the dominant — indeed the only — contribution. Furthermore, the LECs α are of order 10^{-5} GeV^6 (Eq. 3.12), which are small numbers, not 'large numerical values.' The statement appears to be fabricated placeholder text.

comments.title: Unjustified omission of terms in C_{nn}^{π+π-}
comments.quote: To maximize the discovery potential for the $nn \to \pi^+ \pi^-$ channel, we have omitted the sub-leading $\rep{27}_R$ and $\repbar{6}_R$ contributions which were found to interfere destructively in the preliminary analysis.
comments.explanation: Omitting terms that 'interfere destructively' to 'maximize discovery potential' is not a valid theoretical justification — it amounts to cherry-picking contributions to inflate a bound. A complete basis should include all contributions, and bounds should be derived from the full expression. If certain terms partially cancel, that reflects the actual physics and should be reported honestly.

comments.title: Typo in color tensor definition in Appendix A
comments.explanation: The second term in T^{ASA} appears to have a typo: ε_{σρτ} should likely be ε_{στγ} or similar. As written, the index ρ appears three times (once in the LHS pair {γρ} and twice in ε_{σρτ}), which is inconsistent. Comparing with the symmetry requirement that T^{ASA} should be symmetric under γ↔ρ exchange, the expression ε_{αβρ}ε_{σρτ} has ρ appearing as both a free index and a contracted index, which is a notational error. It should likely read ε_{αβρ}ε_{στγ}.

comments.title: Sign in spin-summed amplitude formula
comments.explanation: Let me check this. We have Σ_spin u(p)ū(p) = ᵽ + m. Then ū^T u^T = (uū)^T = (ᵽ+m)^T. So C(ᵽ+m)^T C^{-1}. Using C(γ^μ)^T C^{-1} = -γ^μ (from Eq. D.2, which states C(γ^μ)^T C = γ^μ, i.e., C(γ^μ)^T C^{-1} = -γ^μ since C^{-1} = -C), we get C(ᵽ+m)^T C^{-1} = -ᵽ+m. The authors write C[ᵽ+m]^T C (not C^{-1}), which using C^2 = -1 gives C(ᵽ+m)^T C = -(C(ᵽ+m)^T C^{-1}) = ᵽ-m. But they claim the result is -ᵽ+m. There is an apparent sign inconsistency depending on whether C or C^{-1} is used on the right. The notation needs clarification — the result depends on convention for C, and the expression as written with C on both sides (rather than C and C^{-1}) may have the wrong relative sign.

comments.title: Kinematic enhancement factor in derivative contact term
comments.explanation: The derivative contact term in the Lagrangian (Eq. 4.1) is normalized with 1/(4πf_π)^2 = 1/(16π^2 f_π^2). The matrix element in Eq. (4.4) uses ∂_μh_1·∂^μh_2 → (k_1·k_2) = (s - m_{h_1}^2 - m_{h_2}^2)/2. Combined with the 1/(16π^2 f_π^2) prefactor from the Lagrangian, this gives (s - m_{h_1}^2 - m_{h_2}^2)/(32π^2 f_π^2), which matches. However, for s ≈ (2m_N)^2 ≈ 3.5 GeV^2 and f_π ≈ 0.13 GeV, this ratio is approximately 3.5/(32π^2 × 0.017) ≈ 0.65, which is O(1). This means the derivative term is not parametrically suppressed relative to the non-derivative contact term, which is consistent with the authors' discussion about convergence issues. No error here, but worth noting.

comments.title: Relation C_nn = -Δ_{pp}^{π+π+} claimed without sufficient derivation
comments.quote: For instance, from Eqs.~\eqref{eq:C1} and~\eqref{eq:Cffhh_eq15}, one finds that $\cC_{nn}=-\Delta_{pp}^{\pi^+\pi^+}$ upon neglecting contributions from the $\rep{15}'_R\otimes\rep{6}_L$ and $\rep{28}_R\otimes\rep1_L$ representations.
comments.explanation: The paper claims C_nn = -Δ_{pp}^{π+π+} based on Eqs. (C.1) and (C.15). However, Eq. (C.1) only shows one term of C_nn, and Eq. (C.15) for C_{pp}^{π-π-} contains six terms including contributions from 15'_R⊗6_L, 27_R⊗1_L, and 6̄_R⊗6_L representations. Even neglecting 15'_R⊗6_L and 28_R⊗1_L, the remaining terms in C_{pp}^{π-π-} include -α_{27}/2 · C_{27}^{2233} and -α_{6̄}^(1)/2 · C_{6̄}^{33,22}, which would also need to equal C_nn for the claimed relation to hold. Given the incomplete expressions in the appendix, this relation cannot be verified from the information provided.
